title: Taiwanese chipmaker Nanya plans $6 billion in spending in 2027, riding AI boom
gist: Taiwanese memory chipmaker Nanya Technology plans to roughly quadruple capital spending to over T$200 billion (~$6.2 billion) in 2027 to build a new plant, after reporting Q2 revenue up 684% and net income up 1,324% year-over-year, as AI-driven memory demand and a persistent supply shortage reshape the memory industry's long-term outlook.

insights.claim: Nanya Technology reported unaudited second-quarter revenue of T$82.55 billion, up 684% from a year earlier, with net income surging 1,324% to T$50.19 billion, and gross margin improving to 79.5% from a negative 20.6% a year earlier.
insights.speaker: J
insights.so_what: An extreme swing from negative gross margin to 79.5% in one year is a striking real-time indicator of how severe the AI-driven memory supply shortage has become, useful as a leading proxy for pricing power across the memory sector (Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron).
insights.quote: Lee was speaking after Nanya reported unaudited second-quarter revenue of T$82.55 billion, up 684% from a year earlier. The company's net income surged 1,324% to T$50.19 billion, while gross margin improved to 79.5% from a negative 20.6% a year earlier.
insights.locator: Lee was speaking after Nanya reported

insights.claim: Nanya's customers include Nvidia, Qualcomm, and Google, directly tying its memory business to major AI compute buyers.
insights.speaker: J
insights.so_what: Confirms Nanya sits directly in the AI hardware supply chain, meaning its capex and revenue trajectory serve as a real-time indicator for AI infrastructure demand strength.
insights.quote: Nanya, whose customers include Nvidia (NVDA.O), opens new tab, Qualcomm (QCOM.O), opens new tab and Google (GOOGL.O), opens new tab

insights.claim: Nanya has a market value of around $47 billion.
insights.speaker: J
insights.so_what: Anchors Nanya's valuation for comparison against the scale of its planned $6.2B capex commitment (roughly 13% of market cap in a single year).
insights.quote: Shares in Nanya, which has a market value of around $47 billion

insights.claim: Nanya plans capital spending of more than T$200 billion ($6.2 billion) in 2027, roughly four times this year's spending figure, to help ramp up a new plant, though the budget has not yet received board approval.
insights.speaker: J
insights.so_what: A 4x capex jump signals aggressive capacity build-out in anticipation of sustained AI memory demand; investors should watch for board approval as a confirmation catalyst and monitor whether peers (Samsung, SK Hynix) match this pace.
insights.quote: Taiwanese memory chipmaker Nanya Technology (2408.TW), opens new tab said on Friday it plans capital spending ‌of more than T$200 billion ($6.2 billion) next year, roughly four times this year's figure

insights.claim: Nanya expects to spend more than T$50 billion this year, versus total investment in the new plant reaching about T$480 billion at full production capacity.
insights.speaker: J
insights.so_what: The T$480B full-capacity figure frames the scale of Nanya's total multi-year commitment, useful for modeling long-run capital intensity in the memory sector.
insights.quote: Nanya, whose customers include Nvidia (NVDA.O)... expects to spend more than T$50 billion this year, Lee said. Total investment in the new plant will reach about T$480 billion at full production capacity

insights.claim: The new plant's first phase is scheduled to reach capacity of 30,000 wafers per month in 2028, eventually expanding to 45,000 wafers per month.
insights.speaker: J
insights.so_what: Concrete wafer-capacity targets and timeline give investors a way to track execution against plan and estimate incremental memory supply hitting the market by 2028.
insights.quote: The first phase of the new plant is scheduled to reach capacity of 30,000 wafers per month in 2028, eventually expanding to 45,000 wafers per month.
insights.locator: The first phase of the new plant

insights.claim: Nanya's president said structural changes driven by AI support a stronger long-term outlook for the memory industry, and the current supply shortage is expected to persist for several more quarters.
insights.speaker: J
insights.so_what: A management prediction of a multi-quarter continued memory shortage supports a bullish near-to-medium-term pricing thesis for memory makers broadly, not just Nanya.
insights.quote: Lee said structural changes driven by artificial intelligence were supporting a stronger long-term outlook for the ⁠memory industry, adding that the current supply shortage was expected to persist for several more quarters.

insights.claim: Global memory makers including Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are also ramping up investment to meet surging AI-driven memory demand, indicating an industry-wide capacity race, not an isolated Nanya move.
insights.speaker: J
insights.so_what: Confirms broad-based capex escalation across the memory industry, which could eventually risk oversupply once new capacity comes online circa 2028, a key countervailing signal to watch for the AI memory supercycle thesis.
insights.quote: Global memory makers, including Samsung Electronics (005930.KS), opens new tab and SK Hynix (000660.KS), opens new tab, are ramping up investment to meet ⁠surging AI-driven memory demand.
